title: Bleekeria littoralis
taxonomy: Bleekeria littoralis is a species of fish in the family Cichlidae. It is endemic to Lake Tanganyika, Africa. The Bleekeria littoralis is classified under the kingdom Animalia, phylum Chordata, class Actinopterygii, order Perciformes, and family Cichlidae. The genus Bleekeria is a group of fish within the family Cichlidae, and the species littoralis is one of its members. This species is often referred to by its common name, the Lake Tanganyika sardine.

intro: Bleekeria littoralis, also known as Baeloa or Asian Bleekeria, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing pain and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Bleekeria littoralis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
